message: Internal shorthand — readers outside the company may not understand this.
Internal shorthand — "service disruption" is clearer for external audiences.
"Alot" is not a word — "a lot" is always two words.
Use "all right" — "alright" is incorrect per Salesforce style.
Drop the trailing "s" — use the American form.
Use the preferred spelling: "canceled" or "canceling" (one L).
Use the preferred spelling: "cancellation" (two L's).
Don't use "and/or." Rewrite to clarify — usually "or" (which can mean either or both) works.
Avoid "i.e." in running text — use "that is" instead.
Avoid "e.g." in running text — use "for example" instead.
Avoid "etc." in running text — use "and so on" instead.
Use "advisor" (with -or), not "adviser."
Use lowercase with periods: "a.m." and "p.m." — not A.M., P.M., AM, PM, am, or pm.
Use "okay" — not "OK" or "O.K."
Use "email" — no hyphen.
Use "ecommerce" — no hyphen.

Use "among" — not "amongst."
Use "amid" — not "amidst."
Use "anyway" — not "anyways."

"Prior to" can usually be replaced by "before."

Use the American spelling: "center" — not "centre."
Use "doughnut" — not "donut" (except the Donut app).
Use "whoa" — not "woah."
It's two words: "never mind."
It's two words: "skill set."
Use "e-book" — hyphenated, lowercase B.
Use "click-throughs" — not "click-thrus."
Don't use ordinals in dates — write "April 15" not "April 15th."
Use an em dash (—) instead of a double hyphen (--).
Avoid "We are excited to announce" — focus on the customer, not us.
